title: vicia sepium var. angustifolia
intro: Vicia sepium var. angustifolia, also known as narrow-leaved bitter vetch, is a plant species that belongs to the legume family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as a natural diuretic. Vicia sepium var. angustifolia is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, which can be consumed to harness its potential health benefits.
